overview: This Pioneer archetype blends token generation with traditional control elements to stabilize and outvalue opponents. By leveraging Enduring Innocence for recursion and board wipes, you protect your token engines while setting up lethal Yorion, Sky Nomad attacks. Caretaker's Talent serves as a persistent value sink that punishes aggressive strategies and fuels your late-game dominance.
win_conditions: Yorion, Sky Nomad dealing combat damage with token support
Caretaker's Talent generating card advantage to outdraw opponents
Enduring Innocence enabling recursive board states and value loops

keep_criteria: Hands with early token generators or one-drops
Draws containing Enduring Innocence or Caretaker's Talent
Combinations that enable turn-two Yorion attacks
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one early interaction spell (Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares, or similar), and a value generator (Caretaker's Talent, Enduring Innocence, or a cheap creature).
Prioritize hands that can stabilize by turn 2-3 and have a clear path to casting Yorion by turn 4-5.
mulligan_criteria: High mana counts without early interaction
Hands lacking token producers or Yorion
Draws with only late-game finishers and no ramp
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or 0-1 lands with no interaction.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and one-drops to establish token presence
Use early interaction to disrupt opponent's game plan
Look for turn-two Yorion attacks if possible
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Caretaker's Talent if possible to draw a card and generate a token, or hold interaction for key threats.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Enduring Innocence if you control a creature, or develop a cheap creature.
Sequence lands to ensure untapped mana for Yorion by turn 4-5.
Prioritize playing lands that enter untapped and avoid overextending into potential sweepers.
mid_game: Activate Caretaker's Talent to stabilize against aggressive decks
Cycle through Enduring Innocence to recover key permanents
Build a wide board of tokens to pressure life totals
Preserve interaction for high-impact threats like planeswalkers, key creatures, and combo pieces.
Use Yorion's +1 to cycle through your deck and find answers, but avoid overusing it if you're already ahead on board.
Don't overcommit to board wipes unless necessary
prefer targeted removal to preserve your own tokens.
Manage your graveyard by being aware of recursion threats and timing your removal accordingly.
Commit threats only when you have answered their removal or when you're ahead on board.
late_game: Commit Yorion to the board for massive combat damage
Use Caretaker's Talent to draw into answers or finishers
Leverage Enduring Innocence to replay threats and close games
Yorion, Sky Nomad (via -1 for a token army or +1 for card advantage), aerial threats, or combat tricks.
Win by out-valuing through multiple turns of Yorion activations and token generation, forcing opponents to trade resources inefficiently.
Go all-in when you have answered their removal, have Yorion on board, and can generate enough tokens to overwhelm their blockers or pressure their life total.
Also commit when you have a clear advantage in card advantage and they have no sweepers or interaction left.

key_cards.name: Yorion, Sky Nomad
key_cards.role: Primary finisher and combat damage engine
key_cards.notes: Attack with as many tokens as possible to maximize damage; keep it safe until you have a wide board.

key_cards.name: Caretaker's Talent
key_cards.notes: Activate it when opponents attack or cast spells to draw cards; use it to outvalue control decks and stabilize against aggro.

key_cards.name: Enduring Innocence
key_cards.notes: Exile key permanents to replay them later; use it to bounce opponent's threats or recover your own token generators.

sequencing_tips: Hold up mana for Yorion's attack before casting other spells
Activate Caretaker's Talent in response to opponent's combat or spells
Time Enduring Innocence activations to protect your board or disrupt key turns
Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board too early, failing to use Yorion's +1 efficiently, miscounting life totals when activating -1, and not tracking opponent's interaction.
Unique interactions: Enduring Innocence can be cast for free if you control a creature, making it a powerful early play.
Caretaker's Talent draws a card, so sequence it before Yorion to maximize value.
Yorion's -1 creates tokens that can be used for blocking, so don't just use them for offense.
Always be mindful of the format's specific meta shifts and adjust your sideboard accordingly.

matchups.advice: Focus on early token production and activate Caretaker's Talent to draw into answers.
Use Enduring Innocence to bypass counterspells and generate value with Caretaker's Talent.

Use multiple low-cost threats, cast Yorion when they're tapped out, use instant-speed interaction to clear the way, and consider casting spells in response to their counters to force trades. Prioritize casting Yorion's -1 to generate tokens, which are harder to counter than single threats.

Answer their mana accelerants early, use board wipes to reset their advantage, and race with Yorion tokens if they go wide. If they have a late-game finisher, use Yorion's +1 to out-draw them and find removal.

Win the card advantage war, use Yorion's +1 to out-draw them, and be more willing to trade removal for their value engines. Prioritize keeping interaction to answer their Yorion, and track their sideboard for anti-value cards.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Day of Judgment, Wrath of God, etc.), key removal, and combo pieces.
Note when opponents use their interaction on your early threats versus holding it for Yorion.
Track their graveyard if they play recursion, and monitor their mana development to predict when they can cast their win conditions.
Pay attention to their sideboard choices post-board.
